response.justification: Strong software engineering fundamentals and current AI-focused role at Acme Software show promising trajectory. However, the resume lacks explicit evidence of 8+ years building AI-driven solutions. The candidate's AI experience appears to begin recently (April 2025) with MCP infrastructure work, while prior roles focused on web, mobile, and backend systems. No demonstrated expertise in required GenAI technologies including vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM optimization, or data pipeline design for AI workloads. Python is not listed despite being explicitly required.
strengths: Currently building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators at Acme, showing active engagement with production agentic workflows
Proven staff-level engineering leadership with strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, mentorship across organizations)
Full-stack capability with TypeScript expertise and demonstrated ability to evaluate and adopt appropriate technologies
concerns: AI focus is very recent; 8+ year GenAI background not credibly demonstrated—career appears primarily web/backend focused until current role
No demonstrated expertise with critical GenAI technologies: vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM performance optimization, or evaluation pipelines
Python not listed in skills despite being a required language; cloud experience limited to AWS CDK with no evidence of GCP, Azure, or large-scale data pipeline infrastructure

key_factors.factor: Current AI-agent infrastructure role at Acme
Missing core GenAI technical stack (RAG, vector DBs, LLM optimization)
